'''David Armstrong''' (born 9 April 2001) is an Australian professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|fb}} for the [[Leigh Leopards]] in the [[Super League]]. 

He previously played for the [[Newcastle Knights]] in the [[National Rugby League]].

==Background==
Born in [[Moree, New South Wales]], Armstrong played his junior rugby league for the [[Mungindi Grasshoppers]] before moving to [[Goondiwindi, Queensland]] with his family when he was 12.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-news-2024-david-armstrong-nrl-debut-kalyn-ponga-injury-return-date-who-will-play-fullback-for-newcastle-knights/news-story/d8c1d81adf0a97560078f2a0facea5d1|title=The ‘lightning quick’ Knights rookie set to fill Ponga’s big shoes|date=26 April 2024|website=Fox Sports}}</ref> He finished his schooling as a [[Boarding school|boarder]] at [[Toowoomba Grammar School]] and was a talented rugby union player.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.couriermail.com.au/news/grammar-students-plough-ahead-with-agriculture-trial/news-story/c8b7f962928403286ee6e9aad5f85bc9|title=Grammar students plough ahead with agriculture trial|date=20 March 2018|website=Courier Mail}}</ref>

==Playing career==

===Early years===
In 2019, Armstrong joined the [[Redcliffe Dolphins]], playing for their [[Mal Meninga Cup]] and [[Hastings Deering Colts]] teams.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.couriermail.com.au/questnews/moreton/redcliffe-dolphins-ready-to-take-on-wynnum-manly-in-herald-cup-battle/news-story/701c8f117e08cb3d0f214c6422f0f4de|title=Redcliffe Dolphins ready to take on Wynnum Manly in Herald Cup battle|date=20 March 2019|website=Courier Mail}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://leagueunlimited.com/news/leagueunlimited-mal-meninga-cup-teams-2019-round-5/|title=QRL U18 Auswide Bank Mal Meninga Cup - 2019 Round 5|date=2 April 2019|website=League Unlimited}}</ref>

In 2020, Armstrong signed with the [[Newcastle Knights]] and was set to play for their [[Jersey Flegg Cup]] team before the season was cancelled due to COVID-19.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.thechronicle.com.au/sport/revealed-where-trl-star-david-armstrong-will-play-this-weekend/news-story/a7690c9a1b5154ff60ec896418666532|title=Where TRL star David Armstrong will play this weekend|date=24 April 2024|website=The Chronicle}}</ref> He returned to Queensland, playing for the Goondiwindi Boars in the [[Toowoomba Rugby League]], where he scored a hat trick in their 2021 Grand Final win over Highfields.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://warwicktoday.com.au/sport/2021-09-26/huge-crowds-witness-epic-grand-final/|title=Huge crowds witness epic grand final|date=26 September 2021|website=Warwick Today}}</ref>

In 2022, Armstrong returned to Newcastle, playing for their Jersey Flegg and [[NSW Cup]] teams.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news/2022/03/23/2022-round-3-nsw-cup-and-jersey-flegg-team-lists/|title=2022 Round 3 NSW Cup and Jersey Flegg team lists|date=23 March 2022|website=Newcastle Knights}}</ref> In 2023, he began training with the club's [[National Rugby League|NRL]] squad and was named on the extended bench for their round 27 match against the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news/2023/08/29/dragons-v-knights-round-27-nrl-team-list-thrifty/|title=Dragons v Knights Round 27 NRL Team List|date=29 August 2023|website=Newcastle Knights}}</ref>

===2024===
In 2024, Armstrong joined Newcastle's NRL top 30 squad on a one-year contract.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2024/01/01/2024-nrl-signings-tracker-latest-signings-news-contracts-and-details/|title=2024 NRL Signings Tracker: Finucane hangs up the boots|date=23 April 2024|website=National Rugby League}}</ref> In round 8 of the [[2024 NRL season]], he made his NRL debut for Newcastle against the [[Dolphins (NRL)|Dolphins]],<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news/2024/04/23/dolphins-v-knights-round-8-nrl-team-list/|title=Dolphins v Knights Round 8 NRL team list|date=23 April 2024|website=Newcastle Knights}}</ref> scoring a try on debut.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2024/04/28/report-dolphins-v-knights/|title=Knights fend off late surge to beat Dolphins|date=28 April 2024|website=National Rugby League}}</ref>

In round 11, Armstrong scored a [[hat-trick]] in the clubs 28-24 victory over the [[Gold Coast Titans|Gold Coast]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://au.sports.yahoo.com/armstrong-hat-trick-propels-knights-082700205.html/|title=Armstrong hat-trick propels Knights to thrilling win as Titans fume over late Bunker call|website=au.sports.yahoo.com}}</ref>
On 11 June, Armstrong handed in a transfer request stating he wished to leave the Newcastle club.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.totalrl.com/newcastle-knights-starlet-david-armstrong-set-for-super-league-move-after-handing-in-transfer-request/|title=Newcastle Knights starlet David Armstrong set for Super League move after handing in transfer request|website=www.totalrl.com}}</ref>

In June, it was revealed that Armstrong would be joining English [[Super League]] side [[Leigh Leopards]] in 2025 on a three-year deal.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news/2024/06/26/club-statement-david-armstrong/|title=Club Statement: David Armstrong|date=26 June 2024|website=Newcastle Knights}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|website=BBC Sport|date=26 Jun 2024|accessdate=3 Oct 2024|title=Leigh sign Knights full-back Armstrong for 2025|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/czvvx2lr5g4o}}</ref>

===2025===
Armstrong made his club debut for Leigh in round 1 of the 2025 Super League season against rivals [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]]. Leigh would win the match 1-0 in extra-time after the game finished 0-0 at the conclusion of 80 minutes.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c2k5w971j35o|title=Leigh beat Wigan in extra time after historic 0-0 draw|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
Armstrong played only eleven matches for Leigh in the 2025 Super League season and did not feature in their playoffs campaign.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/3-key-areas-that-saw-leigh-leopards-miss-out-on-old-trafford-as-grand-final-opportunity-passed-up/|title=3 key areas that saw Leigh Leopards miss out on Old Trafford as Grand Final opportunity passed up|website=www.loverugbyleague.com}}</ref>
